     Sources: src/demos/, src/content/components/, src/data/features.ts -->

# Dialog

A modal with entry/exit animations, backdrop blur, light dismiss, focus trapping and Esc-to-close. Zero JavaScript — the button opens it declaratively.

```html
<button
  type="button"
  commandfor="dlg-delete"
  command="show-modal"
  class="inline-flex h-9 cursor-pointer items-center justify-center gap-2 rounded-lg border border-input bg-transparent px-4 text-sm font-medium whitespace-nowrap text-foreground transition-colors hover:bg-accent focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-2 focus-visible:outline-ring"
>
  Open dialog
</button>

<dialog
  id="dlg-delete"
  closedby="any"
  aria-labelledby="dlg-delete-title"
  aria-describedby="dlg-delete-desc"
  class="m-auto w-[min(92vw,26rem)] translate-y-2.5 scale-[.97] rounded-xl border border-border bg-card p-6 text-card-foreground opacity-0 shadow-2xl overscroll-none transition-all transition-discrete duration-200 backdrop:bg-black/0 backdrop:backdrop-blur-none backdrop:overflow-hidden backdrop:overscroll-contain backdrop:transition-all backdrop:transition-discrete backdrop:duration-200 open:translate-y-0 open:scale-100 open:opacity-100 open:backdrop:bg-black/45 open:backdrop:backdrop-blur-xs starting:open:translate-y-2.5 starting:open:scale-[.97] starting:open:opacity-0 starting:open:backdrop:bg-black/0 starting:open:backdrop:backdrop-blur-none"
>
  <h3 id="dlg-delete-title" class="text-base font-semibold tracking-tight">Delete project?</h3>
  <p id="dlg-delete-desc" class="mt-1 mb-5 text-sm text-muted-foreground">
    This removes the project and all of its deployments. This can't be undone.
  </p>
  <div class="flex justify-end gap-2">
    <button
      type="button"
      commandfor="dlg-delete"
      command="close"
      class="inline-flex h-9 cursor-pointer items-center justify-center gap-2 rounded-lg border border-input bg-transparent px-4 text-sm font-medium whitespace-nowrap text-foreground transition-colors hover:bg-accent focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-2 focus-visible:outline-ring"
    >
      Cancel
    </button>
    <button
      type="button"
      commandfor="dlg-delete"
      command="close"
      class="inline-flex h-9 cursor-pointer items-center justify-center gap-2 rounded-lg bg-destructive px-4 text-sm font-medium whitespace-nowrap text-white transition-opacity hover:opacity-90 focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-2 focus-visible:outline-destructive"
    >
      Delete
    </button>
  </div>
</dialog>
```

## How it works

- Native `<dialog>` gives you the top layer, focus trapping, inert background and Esc-to-close
  for free.
- `commandfor` + `command="show-modal"` on the trigger opens it **declaratively**. This invoker
  commands API replaces the `dialog.showModal()` call you'd otherwise write in JavaScript.
- `closedby="any"` adds light dismiss: clicking the backdrop closes it. If a stray click must
  not dismiss it (destructive confirms), use the [Alert Dialog](/components/alert-dialog) instead.
- Entry animation: `starting:open:*` (that's `@starting-style`) defines where the dialog animates
  *from*. Exit animation: the base classes are the closed state, and `transition-discrete`
  (`transition-behavior: allow-discrete`) keeps `display` alive until the transition finishes.
- The backdrop animates the same way via `backdrop:`, including a `backdrop-blur`.
- The one thing native modals *don't* give you is a scroll lock: the page behind is inert but
  still scrolls. `overscroll-none` on the dialog plus `backdrop:overflow-hidden backdrop:overscroll-contain`
  on the backdrop keep the scroll from chaining out — `none` rather than `contain` so the dialog's
  own scroll doesn't rubber-band at its edges either. Needs Chrome 144+ (where `overscroll-behavior`
  starts applying to non-scrollable containers); everywhere else the background scrolls, nothing breaks.
- Tailwind v4's preflight zeroes all margins, which removes the browser's built-in dialog
  centering. `m-auto` puts it back so keep it.

## Accessibility

Everything the JS dialog libraries reimplement (focus trap, `aria-modal`, inert page behind,
focus restore on close) is the browser's native behavior here. The one thing `<dialog>` does
*not* supply is an accessible name: point `aria-labelledby` at the title (and `aria-describedby`
at the body text) like the demo does, so screen readers announce what the dialog is asking.

## Leading-edge features used (and what happens without them)

- **<dialog> element** ([MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Elements/dialog))
  Without it: Baseline, widely available. Modal behavior, focus handling and Esc-to-close are native.
- **Invoker commands (commandfor / command)** ([MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Invoker_Commands_API))
  Without it: Buttons with commandfor do nothing. Until Baseline reaches your audience, open the dialog with a 3-line shim or from your framework.
- **closedby="any" (light dismiss)** ([MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Elements/dialog#closedby))
  Without it: Esc still closes the dialog (platform default); clicking the backdrop does not.
- **Entry/exit animations (allow-discrete + @starting-style)** ([MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/transition-behavior))
  Without it: Elements appear and disappear instantly. Nothing breaks — you just lose the animation.
